sections.context: The L5 uplift mission has now ported the toolkit (Milestone 0) and uplifted the five sub-repos (canopy, plot, mulch, seeds, burrow) to Level 5. The final milestone seals the os-eco META repo itself: it has no package.json, no test suite, no lint pipeline, and ships no code — but the auditor still inspects governance hygiene (AGENTS.md, .gitignore, .github/ scaffolding, label taxonomy, toolkit discoverability) at the root. Currently the root has minimal .github/ (only auto-merge.yml + an EMPTY ISSUE_TEMPLATE directory), no AGENTS.md, no dependabot.yml, no labels.yml, no sync-labels workflow, and a thin .gitignore. This feature closes those gaps so the final /readiness-report can declare the meta-repo at Level 5, completing VAL-OS-ECO-001..007 + FINAL and feeding the cross-area VAL-CROSS-001..006 invariants.
sections.approach: Close the four governance/discoverability gaps for the os-eco META repo (no sub-repos touched) by porting the relevant artifacts out of templates/l5-toolkit/github/ into the os-eco root .github/ tree, hardening the root .gitignore, and pointing top-level docs at the L5 toolkit. Order is strict: governance files first, then gitignore + labels, then toolkit discoverability, then the milestone seal. The seal commit is the final wrap-up of the whole L5 uplift mission across the ecosystem.

steps.title: Extend root .gitignore and add labels.yml + sync-labels workflow
Make L5 toolkit discoverable from root docs
Finalize for audit: verify governance, request /readiness-report

acceptance: AGENTS.md exists at /Users/jayminwest/Projects/os-eco/AGENTS.md (>2KB) with an Ecosystem section documenting the eight sub-repos and the cross-cutting .seeds/.mulch/.canopy/.plot/ directories.
.github/dependabot.yml exists and declares a weekly github-actions ecosystem with a cooldown block (no npm ecosystem block — root has no package.json).
.github/pull_request_template.md exists at os-eco root with Summary / Changes / Test plan sections.
Root .gitignore preserves the existing sub-repo + .run-plan-logs ignores AND adds .env, .env.* (with !.env.example), .idea/, .vscode/, .DS_Store, *.log.
.github/labels.yml exists with the canonical P0–P3 priority/area/kind/status taxonomy AND .github/workflows/sync-labels.yml exists and mirrors warren's pattern.
README.md or CLAUDE.md at the os-eco root contains a clearly-marked L5 Toolkit section linking to templates/l5-toolkit/README.md.
git status clean on os-eco main after all four children commit; the four feature commits each name the VAL-OS-ECO-* criteria they close in their messages.
The final child's salient handoff instructs the user to run /readiness-report from /Users/jayminwest/Projects/os-eco/ to produce .factory-mission-scratch/os-eco-readiness-report.md.

sections.context: The eight os-eco sub-repos run divergent quality gates. warren uses a 12-gate && chain with verbose names (check:file-sizes, check:debt-markers, check:duplicates, validate:agents-md) plus a check:ci-parity gate it originated; plot/mulch/seeds/trellis use the terse l5-toolkit names (check:size/debt/dups/deps/agents/coverage) in plain && chains; canopy omits check:coverage from its chain; burrow and sapling have no check:all at all (only test/lint/typecheck). No repo has a quiet runner, a `verify` alias, or (except warren) ci-parity. The sibling kota-monorepo solved this exact drift by freezing a canonical check:all standard: an ordered gate list with one canonical name per gate, a byte-identical scripts/check-all.ts quiet runner (one aligned line per gate, signatures-only on failure), `verify` aliased to check:all everywhere, and a check:ci-parity meta-gate (itself ported FROM warren) that fails any CI step not reachable from check:all. os-eco already owns the seed of this in templates/l5-toolkit (canonical terse names + check:* scripts) but stopped short of a frozen standard, the quiet runner, the verify alias, and fleet-wide coverage. This root tracker holds the cross-repo foundation (the standard doc + the portable runner/ci-parity artifacts in templates/l5-toolkit) and coordinates the eight per-repo conformance plans that consume it.
sections.approach: Land the cross-repo foundation here, then let each repo conform via its own sd plan (warren, burrow, plot, mulch, seeds, canopy, sapling, trellis). Step 1 writes docs/check-all-standard.md freezing the canonical terse vocabulary (matching templates/l5-toolkit precedent, with warren renaming its verbose keys), the ordered core gate set, a conditional-gate registry (check:bundle-size, gen:docs:check, gen:openapi:check), the quiet-output contract, the verify rule, and the ci-parity rule. Step 2 builds the portable quiet runner + generalized ci-parity into templates/l5-toolkit/scripts (with tests) and updates package-scripts.json + the toolkit README, porting warren's existing scripts/check-ci-parity.ts as the reference. Step 3 is the fleet close-out: once the eight per-repo plans land, verify byte-identical runners + canonical names + green ci-parity + quiet output across all repos, update root CLAUDE.md, and record the ecosystem decision. Cross-store sd dependencies can't be expressed, so the per-repo plans reference these foundational artifacts in prose; this tracker is the coordination point.

alternatives.name: One root plan that edits all eight repos directly
alternatives.rejected_because: Each sub-repo has its own .seeds/ store and git history; warren's monorepo model is one-branch-one-PR per repo, so conformance work belongs in each repo's own plan, not a single cross-repo plan that can't open eight PRs.

alternatives.name: Keep warren's verbose gate names as canonical and rename the other repos
alternatives.rejected_because: templates/l5-toolkit already documents the terse names (check:size/debt/dups/deps/agents/coverage) as canonical and four repos already use them; renaming one repo (warren) is far less churn than renaming five.

steps.title: Author docs/check-all-standard.md at the os-eco root: freeze the canonical terse gate vocabulary (check:size/debt/dups/deps/agents/coverage + lint/typecheck), the ordered core gate list (lint, typecheck, check:agents, check:dups, check:deps, check:size, check:debt, [conditional gates], check:coverage, check:ci-parity LAST), a conditional-gate registry (check:bundle-size for bundle producers, gen:docs:check for doc generators, gen:openapi:check for OpenAPI servers), the quiet-output contract (one aligned `<status> <gate> (N.Ns)` line per gate; on failure only failing gate names + parsed test signatures; CHECK_ALL_VERBOSE/--bail escape hatches), the `verify` = `bun run check:all` rule, and the check:ci-parity rule. Include a per-repo reconciliation table mapping each repo's current names to canonical (esp. warren's verbose keys). Doc-only; changes no repo behavior.

Build the portable quiet runner + generalized ci-parity in templates/l5-toolkit/scripts: add check-all.ts (exports a GATES array run as data, captures each gate's output, prints the aligned per-gate lines + tally on success and signatures-only on failure, honors CHECK_ALL_VERBOSE and --bail) and check-ci-parity.ts (imports GATES from check-all.ts, parses .github/workflows/ci*.yml, fails on any `bun run <x>` not reachable from check:all, with ALIASES/CI_ONLY escape hatches) plus co-located *.test.ts; port warren/scripts/check-ci-parity.ts as the reference implementation. Update templates/l5-toolkit/package-scripts.json so check:all becomes `bun scripts/check-all.ts`, add `verify`: `bun run check:all` and `check:ci-parity`: `bun scripts/check-ci-parity.ts`, add the `yaml` devDep. Document the runner + verify + ci-parity wiring in templates/l5-toolkit/README.md.

Fleet close-out and parity verification: after the eight per-repo conformance plans land, run `bun run check:all` and `bun run verify` in every sub-repo and confirm each uses the byte-identical scripts/check-all.ts runner, the canonical gate names in the standard's order, a green check:ci-parity, and the quiet-output contract (one line per gate, signatures-only on failure). Update the root CLAUDE.md 'Build & Test Commands' section to standardize on `bun run check:all` / `bun run verify`, and record an ecosystem-domain mulch decision capturing the adopted standard.

risks: Cross-store sd plans can't hard-link dependencies, so the per-repo plans depend on this tracker's steps 1-2 only by prose convention; landing a repo plan before the standard/runner exists would create rework.
The standard's name reconciliation (warren verbose -> terse) touches CLAUDE.md and CI references; missing a reference leaves a dangling script name.
acceptance: docs/check-all-standard.md exists at the os-eco root with the canonical ordered gate list, conditional-gate registry, quiet-output contract, verify rule, ci-parity rule, and a per-repo reconciliation table.
templates/l5-toolkit/scripts/check-all.ts and check-ci-parity.ts (with tests) exist and templates/l5-toolkit/package-scripts.json wires check:all -> the runner, plus verify and check:ci-parity.
All eight sub-repos run `bun run check:all` and `bun run verify` green with the byte-identical runner, canonical names, and a passing check:ci-parity.
Root CLAUDE.md documents the standard and an ecosystem mulch decision records it.

name: Adopt canonical check:all standard across the os-eco fleet

sections.context: Forkar um repo da fleet pra rodar a esteira propria exige pre-requisitos de CI/merge que um fork fresco nao tem, e quando faltam os PRs do plan-run travam silenciosamente (o warren so faz polling do merge e da timeout). Ja mordeu: o pl-a77b no fork do warren abriu 2 PRs que nunca mergearam porque a CI NUNCA rodou (GitHub desabilita Actions em fork por padrao; precisa de um clique unico na UI que a API nao destrava). Revisao apos checar o ecossistema: a parte de AUDITORIA de prontidao NAO deve ser construida aqui, ela ja e o trellis (audit & sync, rubrica de 9 categorias, drift de config, audita a fleet inteira) e o notes/readiness-report-prompt.md ja pontua branch_protection e fast_ci_feedback; e os nomes doctor/onboard ja sao usados pelos tools (health interno e init de projeto) e burrow fork e fork de sandbox. O unico gap genuinamente novo e o BOOTSTRAP ao vivo de CI/merge de um fork do GitHub (incluindo a deteccao do passo UI-only de habilitar Actions, que e estado de runtime do GitHub e nao um arquivo, entao a auditoria estatica do trellis nao cobre).
sections.approach: Dois artefatos no root do os-eco (cross-tool). (1) scripts/fork-ci-bootstrap.sh: idempotente, faz o automatizavel via gh/git (re-aponta remotes origin=fork/upstream; sincroniza com upstream; seta AUTO_MERGE_PAT; habilita allow_auto_merge; configura branch protection com required check ci e enforce_admins=false) e APLICA os artefatos canonicos (ci.yml do templates/l5-toolkit, auto-merge.yml do repo) em vez de recriar; e detecta o gap que travou (consulta gh: se runs==0, avisa em destaque que falta habilitar Actions na UI do fork). (2) docs/fork-onboarding.md: runbook com a ordem dos passos, o passo UI-only em destaque, e aponta o trellis (trellis audit/drift/fleet) como o auditor das dimensoes estaticas (branch_protection, fast_ci_feedback, etc.), pra nao duplicar. A auditoria NAO e reimplementada aqui; o criterio live de 'Actions habilitado no fork' fica registrado como proposta pro trellis (seed separado).

alternatives.name: Construir um fork-doctor.sh proprio (era o step 2 original)
alternatives.rejected_because: redundante: a auditoria de prontidao e o trellis (rubrica de 9 categorias, audita a fleet), e o readiness-report ja pontua branch_protection e fast_ci_feedback; alem de colidir com os comandos doctor ja existentes nos tools

alternatives.name: Recriar ci.yml/auto-merge.yml no script
alternatives.rejected_because: ci.yml e canonico no templates/l5-toolkit e o auto-merge.yml ja existe no fork; o script deve APLICAR, nao duplicar

alternatives.name: Tentar automatizar o enable de Actions do fork
alternatives.rejected_because: e UI-only (a API diz enabled=true e os workflows aparecem active, mas nada roda ate o clique no banner); o script DETECTA (runs==0) e instrui, nao finge automatizar

alternatives.name: Botar o check 'Actions habilitado' como criterio estatico do trellis
alternatives.rejected_because: e estado de runtime do GitHub, nao um arquivo; nao cabe numa auditoria estatica. Fica no bootstrap (live) e como proposta de check live ao trellis

risks: O passo de habilitar Actions e UI-only: o script NAO pode fingir que automatiza; detecta runs==0 e instrui o clique, senao da falsa sensacao de pronto
Branch protection mal configurada pode bloquear o dono: enforce_admins=false (dono ainda da push direto) e exigir so o check ci
Idempotencia: rodar 2x nao pode quebrar nem duplicar remotes/secret
AUTO_MERGE_PAT e segredo: recebido via env/stdin, nunca hardcoded nem ecoado
Nao duplicar o trellis: o runbook aponta o trellis para a auditoria; este plano so faz o bootstrap ao vivo que o trellis estatico nao cobre
acceptance: scripts/fork-ci-bootstrap.sh roda idempotente, aplica os artefatos canonicos (ci.yml do l5-toolkit, auto-merge.yml) sem recriar, configura remotes/sync/AUTO_MERGE_PAT/allow_auto_merge/branch-protection (required check ci, enforce_admins=false), e avisa em destaque quando runs==0 (Actions nao habilitado na UI do fork)
docs/fork-onboarding.md descreve a ordem com o passo UI-only em destaque e aponta o trellis como o auditor das dimensoes estaticas (sem reimplementar auditoria)
nenhum fork-doctor novo e criado; a auditoria continua sendo responsabilidade do trellis
script passa shellcheck/teste; gate verde em todos os PRs do plano

name: os-eco: fork CI/merge bootstrap (script + runbook); auditoria de prontidao fica no trellis
